Implemented the ARC2 package for `17cae0c1` in [__init__.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_17cae0c1/__init__.py#L1), [verifier.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_17cae0c1/verifier.py#L1), and [generator.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_17cae0c1/generator.py#L1). The rule is: split the `3x9` input into three `3x3` glyph blocks, recognize each block by its occupied-cell pattern, and replace it with a solid `3x3` block of color `6`, `1`, `9`, `4`, or `3` for top bar, bottom bar, anti-diagonal, center dot, and ring respectively.

I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint because it matched the official examples, so no `notes.md` was needed. No new primitive was added. The generator keeps the same task family by assembling the same canonical glyphs into a `3x9` strip; to meet the shared builder’s `1000`-unique-example requirement, it varies the shared foreground color and allows repeated glyph choices while preserving the same occupancy-based verifier rule.
